model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous regression likely came from an overly aggressive initial learning rate; this keeps the step-aligned cosine schedule but restores the more stable baseline LR to isolate schedule benefit under the 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Recent regressions indicate LR decay is hurting this short fixed-step run; disabling scheduling restores constant-LR behavior while keeping other settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase early feature capacity with a minimal architecture change to improve representational power while keeping depth and training dynamics unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With the stronger 16-channel backbone, a moderately higher constant LR may improve early optimization speed within the strict 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the current stronger model and higher LR, decoupled weight decay from AdamW can improve optimization/regularization balance within the short fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"In the short 256-step setting, slightly weaker regularization with AdamW can reduce underfitting and improve validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"A modestly larger classifier head can better use improved convolutional features with minimal complexity and likely better fit under the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger FC head worked well; increasing convolutional width should further improve feature quality while keeping the architecture simple and stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have been consistently beneficial; expanding the classifier bottleneck further is a simple continuation likely to improve fit within the same fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Performance keeps improving with moderate capacity increases, so another small width increase is a low-complexity next step with high expected upside.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last width increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to the previously better channel width restores the stronger capacity sweet spot before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"A lower AdamW beta2 can adapt second-moment estimates faster, which often helps optimization in very short training horizons like 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The faster-moment variant slightly regressed, so restoring the previous AdamW betas returns to the better-performing optimizer configuration.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed step budget, doubling batch size exposes the model to more unique examples and can improve validation loss without changing optimization logic.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large gain from increasing batch size suggests data coverage is the dominant limiter; raising batch size again should expose even more unique samples within the fixed 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has produced the largest gains so far; increasing to 512 should further improve data coverage per fixed update and may reduce validation loss again.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"At batch size 512, the optimizer can usually support a higher step size; increasing LR should improve progress per update within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"With larger batch and higher LR, a step-aligned cosine decay may now help by combining fast early progress with lower final-step loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay significantly regressed at the current large-batch high-LR setting, so reverting to the stronger constant-LR configuration is the highest-confidence final move.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
